title: ledum palustre subsp. longifolium
intro: Ledum palustre subsp. longifolium, also known as bog rosemary, is a subspecies of the bog rosemary plant, a low-growing evergreen shrub that is native to northern Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ledum palustre subsp. longifolium include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve congestion and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to treat a range of health conditions, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to potential toxicity.
